demographic_audit_note: Audit of Atlantis queue against facility face sheet: Medicare Advantage plan verified. Address confirmed as single-family home. Found DOB discrepancy (1940 vs 1941) and flagged for SW correction.
home_vs_ltc_determination: Social Worker confirmed the goal is Home with 24/7 family support; LTC was ruled out after patient met ICU step-down milestones.
stage_1_intake_responses: Q1: 'No, I haven't heard of you.' Q2: 'I think you're with the insurance company.' Q3: 'The doctor mentioned someone would come to the house.' Q4: 'I'm worried about the stairs and how I'll get home from the curb.' (Escalated mobility concern to SW).
weekly_facility_update: Week 2 Check-in: Patient completing IV antibiotic course for pneumonia. PT reports patient is now at a '1-person assist' for transfers. Target D/C set for Friday.
v_card_and_flyer_status: V-Card successfully inserted into daughter's phone during bedside education; program flyer delivered and reviewed with family.
hha_confirmation_status: In Place. HHA (CenterWell) confirmed SOC for Saturday morning; Nurse Miller assigned; first-visit window 0900-1100 confirmed after initial transport delay resolved.
stage_3_followup_audit: Call 1 (SW): Confirmed Friday D/C is firm; HHA has the orders. Call 2 (Family): Verified daughter understands the 0900 HHA arrival and confirmed hospital bed was delivered to the first floor.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: 24-hour pulse call completed. Reached daughter; confirmed oxygen concentrator is active at home. Transport van confirmed for 1400 Friday discharge.
atlantis_final_sync: Entered D/C date (Friday) and 1st MA visit date (Saturday) into Atlantis system.
ma_visit_booking: MA scheduled for Saturday at 1300. Booking held until HHA confirmed 'In Place' status following the transport coordination resolution.
narrative_summary: Following a post-ICU pneumonia stay, the patient's transition was initially threatened by a transport vendor cancellation for the Friday discharge. During the Connection Intake, the patient expressed high anxiety regarding the physical move back to her home. By performing the Confidence Audit and verifying HHA 'In Place' status before scheduling the MA, the PN ensured the loop was closed despite the transport friction. The first home visit was successfully completed within the 24-hour window.

format_1_state_log.event_description: Atlantis demographic audit reveals DOB mismatch between facility and payer records.
Medical transport (wheelchair van) reports no availability for the scheduled Friday 2 PM discharge window.
HHA refuses to confirm Saturday SOC window until Friday transport is re-secured.

format_2_triples.situation: Connection Intake Q4 surfaces patient fear of stairs and curb-to-bed mobility.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Escalated specific mobility barriers to SW and PT to ensure home safety eval was prioritized.

format_2_triples.situation: Transport vendor cancels Friday pickup, risking a weekend discharge gap.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Conducted Confidence Audit with SW to confirm alternative transport was secured before notifying HHA.

format_2_triples.situation: Daughter expresses confusion about who arrives first on Day 1.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Educated family on the sequence of HHA nurse arrival followed by the MA visit.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Wait for the Social Worker to resolve the transport cancellation and update the HHA before attempting to schedule the MA visit.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Respects the SW's domain over logistics while adhering to the HHA-First rule by not scheduling the MA until the new discharge timeline is firm.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Perform the Confidence Audit by calling the HHA intake coordinator to identify if the transport delay will cause them to lose the Saturday staffing slot.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Gathers critical 'In Place' data to prevent a wasted MA visit while staying within the PN scope of coordination and verification.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Call the transport company directly to demand a priority slot for the patient and then call the HHA to suggest they use a different nursing agency that has better weekend coverage.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Violates the SW boundary regarding transport/agencies and oversteps into HHA selection, which is a banned action.
